scope.summary: Implement the Toon wgpu render pipeline in ClothViewport with configurable shading bands, outline pass, rim lighting, emissive color, and MatCap material mode, gated behind a cargo feature toon-shader per the post-MVP deferral note in Section 13.9
acceptance_criteria: A toon-shader Cargo feature gates the Toon pipeline; the crate compiles and passes all existing tests with the feature disabled
When enabled, RenderMode::Toon selects the toon_cloth.wgsl fragment shader which implements quantized shading bands (configurable band count 2–6), rim lighting, and emissive color addition
A separate outline render pass (toon_outline.wgsl) draws silhouette and crease edges as solid lines; outline width is a ViewportConfig parameter
MatCap material mode samples a MatCap texture (provided as a 256x256 PNG artifact ref) using the view-space normal as UV coordinates in place of the PBR shader
tailor_viewport_config accepts render_mode: 'toon' when the feature is enabled
Visual integration test: a denim jacket in Toon mode with 3 bands shows hard shading transitions; the captured PNG is assertable as non-PBR by absence of specular highlights
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: capture a draped T-shirt in RenderMode::Toon; assert visual output shows banded shading (pixel value histogram shows discrete luminance clusters)
scope.risk_if_missed: Without Toon/MatCap rendering, Tailor cannot produce stylized garment previews for anime, illustration, or game-engine cartoon pipelines. Clients targeting stylized renders must export and re-render in external tools, adding a full DCC roundtrip per preview iteration.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
